title: Centralize Salesforce Instance URL via Laravel Service Container
description:
Centralize Salesforce instance URL retrieval using a Laravel Service Container binding instead of fetching it from the `Users` model in every controller.

### Implementation
- Created a Service Container binding named `salesforce_instance_url`
- Binding retrieves the instance URL from the `SalesforceUserInformation` model
- Accessible throughout the application via `app('salesforce_instance_url')`
- Automatically updates when a customer selects a different shared Salesforce account

### Benefits
- Eliminates scattered model calls across controllers
- Centralizes Salesforce configuration retrieval
- Improves maintainability and extensibility
acceptance_criteria: Salesforce instance URL is accessible via app('salesforce_instance_url')
All controllers use the service container binding instead of direct model calls
URL updates when customer switches Salesforce accounts
middleware_changes: Created Laravel Service Container binding for Salesforce instance URL. Refactored controllers to use app('salesforce_instance_url') instead of direct model queries.
implementation_notes: Service Container binding reads from SalesforceUserInformation model. Provides single-point-of-access pattern for Salesforce configuration.
comment_summary: Waqar documented the Service Container approach and its benefits. Ryan approved.
